title: Gyárfás Conjecture 1.3 (long odd holes)
statement_text: For all $\kappa, \ell \geq 0$ there exists $c$ such that for every graph $G$, if $\omega(G) \leq \kappa$ and $\chi(G) > c$ then $G$ has an odd hole of length at least $\ell$.
context_text: András Gyárfás proposed three conjectures in [4] about the lengths of holes in graphs with large chromatic number and bounded clique number. This third conjecture is the strongest and contains the other two; the first was proved in [5] and the second in [2], but the third remained open until this paper.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture in full by induction on $\kappa$, establishing that for all $\kappa, \ell, \tau$ there exists $c$ such that every $(\kappa, \ell, \tau)$-candidate has chromatic number at most $c$.
notes: Labelled Conjecture 1.3 in the paper; the abstract explicitly states this conjecture is proved. Full bibliographic year of reference [4] not recoverable from the extracted text.
paper_title: Induced subgraphs of graphs with large chromatic number. VIII. Long odd holes

title: Consecutive hole lengths conjecture for all clique numbers
statement_text: For all $\kappa, \ell \geq 0$, there exists $c \geq 0$ such that for every graph $G$, if $\omega(G) \leq \kappa$ and $\chi(G) > c$, then $G$ has holes of $\ell$ consecutive lengths.
context_text: The case $\kappa = 2$ (Theorem 1.5 of the paper) was proved in reference [6]: if $\omega(G) \leq 2$ and $\chi(G) > c$ then $G$ has holes of $\ell$ consecutive lengths. The extension to all fixed $\kappa$ was conjectured in [6] but the paper notes it remains open.
paper_contribution: The authors note they can prove a weaker partial result — that there exist two holes both of length at least $\ell$ with consecutive lengths — by a refinement of the paper's methods, but omit the details.
notes: Conjecture stated in running prose without a numbered environment; full bibliographic details of reference [6] are not recoverable from the extracted text.
paper_title: Induced subgraphs of graphs with large chromatic number. VIII. Long odd holes
